DECLARATION OF MARY CATHERINE WIEDERHOLD IS SUPPORT OF JUDGEMENT CREDITOR KEITH PULLIAMS MOTION FOR AN ORDER ASSIGNING TO HIS ATTORNEYS, LAW OFFICES OF MARY CATHERINE WIEDERHOLD, THE MAY 22, 2024 JUDGMENT, AN ORDER ASSIGNING THE RENTS (TRANSACTION ID # 75660326) FILED BY PLAINTIFF PULLIAM, KEITH

REAL PROPERTY/HOUSING MOTION 501, KEITH PULLIAM'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER ASSIGNING TO HIS ATTORNEYS, LAW OFFICES OF MARY CATHERINE WIEDERHOLD, THE MAY 22, 2024, JUDGMENT, AN ORDER ASSIGNING THE RENTS FROM THE TENANTS AT JUDGMENT DEBTOR'S SAN FRANCISCO RENTAL PROPERTIES AND FOR A RESTRAINING ORDER AGAINST JUDGMENT DEBTOR FRANCISCO'S MEXICAN FOOD, LLC. NO APPEARANCES. THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO CITE ANY AUTHORITY FOR THE PROPOSITION THAT A COURT ORDER IS REQUIRED OR IS PROPER FOR AN ASSIGNMENT UNDER CCP 954. MOVING PARTY FAILS TO PROVIDE ADMISSIBLE EVIDENCE THAT THE JUDGMENT DEBTOR HAS A RIGHT TO PAYMENT OF RENTS GENERATED BY THE THREE PROPERTIES MENTIONED IN THE APPLICATION. PREVAILING PARTY TO SUBMIT A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: HARRY L. JACOBS, CLERK: E. LARNAUTI, NOT REPORTED

Moving party fails to cite any authority for the proposition that a Court order is required or is proper for an assignment under CCP 954. ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Jun-13-2024 Off Calendar

Notice Of Hearing Regarding Plaintiff'S Application For Court Judgment Against Defendant Francisco'S Mexican Food, Llc
Off Calendar May-22-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter came on calendar for request for default judgment against Defendant Francisco's Mexican Food, LLC.

Dept: 505; Judge: Maria Evangelista; Clerk: K. Valdes; Proceedings reported by "For the Record" only to be used in compliance with California Government Code Section 69957, commencing at 9:37:20 a.m. and concluding at 10:28:41 a.m. (D505)

Mary Weiderhold, Esq. representing Plaintiff Keith Pulliam.
Courtney M. Brown, Esq. representing Plaintiff Keith Pulliam.
Plaintiff Keith Pulliam was placed under oath and testified.
Defendant Francisco's Mexican Food, LLC not appearing.
Greg Marell, expert witness, sworn to testify for the Plaintiff.
Michael James, expert witness, sworn to testify for the Plaintiff.
Dr. Phil Allman, expert witness, sworn to testify for the Plaintiff.

Plaintiff's amended complaint for damages filed May 26, 2022.
Proof of personal service as to Defendant Francisco's Mexican Food, LLC on June 24, 2022.
Default entered as to Defendant Francisco's Mexican Food, LLC November 10, 2022.
Dismissal filed as to Defendant Francisco Javier Ramos on October 30, 2023.
Dismissal filed as to Defendant Does 1-50 on October 30, 2023.

Counsel to summarize the causes of action and to offer proof of as to witness testimony.

Plaintiff submits the following exhibits for the Court's review:
Exhibit 1:Photo of Plaintiff's hallway
Exhibit 2:Photo of Plaintiff's kitchen
Exhibit 3:Photo of Plaintiff's storage
Exhibit 4: Photo of Plaintiff's backyard
Exhibit 5:Complaint data sheet from SF Dept. of Building Inspection
Exhibit 6:Inspection request letter dated March 10, 2020 from SF Dept. of Building Inspection
Exhibit 7: Inspection request letter dated March 26, 2020 from SF Dept. of Building Inspection
Exhibit 8:Letter of Petitioner to Defendant's agent Francisco Ramos
Exhibit 9:Marell Inspection Services Report
Exhibit 10:James Appraisal Service certification
Exhibit 11:Dr. Phil Allman's rent differential analysis

The Court takes judicial notice of Exhibit 12: filed documents for the prove up hearing for request of default judgment.
The Court and Counsel discuss the trebling of damages.
Witness Dr. Phil Allman is called to testify.
Witness does not confirm that the rent differential analysis he submitted is true and correct. The valuation given was different. Witness to calculate the rent differential according to amount stated.
Counsel amends the damages and interest sought based on expert calculation.
Witness Michael James is called to testify.
Witness Michael James confirms that the appraisal service certification submitted true and correct to the best of his personal knowledge.
Witness Greg Marell is called to testify.
Witness Greg Marell confirms that the inspection services report submitted is true and correct to the best of his personal knowledge.
Plaintiff is examined by the Court.
Plaintiff testifies and confirms that the statements made by counsel, declarations and reports submitted are true and correct.
Exhibits are accepted by the Court as evidence.

Based on the testimony, declarations and exhibits, the Court finds that the Plaintiff has met their prima facie case according to proof.

The Court rules in favor of the Plaintiff Keith Pulliam and against Defendant Francisco's Mexican Food, LLC. as follows:

Plaintiff shall recover from Defendant:
Damages $275,148.00
Attorney's Fees $0
Costs $906.60
Interest $42,139.10

Total Judgment $318,193.70

Proposed judgment as amended comports to proof and the Court signs and executes judgment this day.
